bundle_inaccuracies.issue: Tool skill describes 'doca-gpi-ib-write-lat' as an in-bundle tool, but the doca/ monorepo's tools/ directory contains NO gpi_ib_write_lat directory; only gpunetio_ib_write_lat and gpunetio_ib_write_bw exist. The bundle even ships a separate (correct) doca-gpunetio-ib-write-lat skill alongside the fabricated one, so this is not a rename. The bundle's own README claims 'strict 1:1 with doca/tools/' and 'never invent paths/package names', which this row directly violates.
bundle_inaccuracies.evidence: ls /Users/ttevet/Documents/git/tmp-proj/mcp-agents/doca/tools/ -> apsh_config bench bench_extension caps comm_channel_admin common dpa_hl_tracer flow_dpa_perf flow_grpc_server flow_perf flow_tune gpunetio_ib_write_bw gpunetio_ib_write_lat pcc_counters sha_offload_engine socket_relay spcx_cc telemetry_utils. find doca/ -name '*gpi_ib_write_lat*' returns 0 hits.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: Remove skills/tools/doca-gpi-ib-write-lat/, drop the row from README.md and SKILLS.md, and either lower the tool count from 18 to 17 in the catalog or audit any other potentially-fabricated tool rows.

bundle_inaccuracies.issue: AGENTS.md cites 'doca_sha_cap_is_algorithm_supported(devinfo, DOCA_SHA_ALGORITHM_SHA256)' as the canonical example of a cap-query call. The real public API in doca/libs/doca_sha/include/public/doca_sha.h is 'doca_sha_cap_task_hash_get_supported(devinfo, algorithm, *supported)'. The enum value DOCA_SHA_ALGORITHM_SHA256 exists; the function name does not.
bundle_inaccuracies.evidence: grep -nE 'doca_sha_cap_' doca/libs/doca_sha/include/public/doca_sha.h -> doca_sha_cap_task_hash_get_supported only; no doca_sha_cap_is_algorithm_supported anywhere in the tree.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: Replace the AGENTS.md example with 'doca_sha_cap_task_hash_get_supported(devinfo, DOCA_SHA_ALGORITHM_SHA256, &supported)'.

bundle_inaccuracies.file: AGENTS.md (Flow cap-query example) + skills/doca-debug/CAPABILITIES.md (## Universal debug-loop contract stacking rule)
bundle_inaccuracies.issue: Both files reference 'doca_flow_aggr_query' as a Flow counters/query API. No such symbol exists in doca/libs/doca_flow/core/include/public/doca_flow.h; the actual Flow query family is doca_flow_resource_query_entry / doca_flow_resource_query_pipe_miss / doca_flow_port_query_default_miss / doca_flow_shared_resources_query.
bundle_inaccuracies.evidence: grep -n 'aggr_query\|doca_flow_aggr' doca/libs/doca_flow/core/include/public/doca_flow.h -> 0 hits; grep -n 'doca_flow_resource_query' on the same header lists 6 matches.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: Replace 'doca_flow_aggr_query' with 'doca_flow_resource_query_entry' (or similar) in AGENTS.md and skills/doca-debug/CAPABILITIES.md.

bundle_inaccuracies.issue: AGENTS.md references a 'doca_flow_cap_get_*' family as the way Flow exposes capability gates. No public Flow header exposes any doca_flow_cap_* prefix; doca_flow.h uses doca_flow_cfg_*, doca_flow_pipe_cfg_*, doca_flow_resource_query_*, etc. The 'cap_get' family appears in other libraries (doca_rdma_cap_task_*, doca_sha_cap_*) but not for doca_flow.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: Either reference a real Flow capability function (doca_flow_cfg_*) or remove the 'for Flow it is doca_flow_cap_get_*' clause; AGENTS.md's anti-fabrication rule #3 forbids this kind of synthesized symbol.

bundle_inaccuracies.issue: The structured-tools-contract names 'doca_flow_pipe_validate' as the per-library validate call the agent should reach for when the structured 'validate-before-commit' helper is absent. There is no doca_flow_pipe_validate function anywhere in doca/libs/doca_flow/**.
bundle_inaccuracies.evidence: grep -RIn 'doca_flow_pipe_validate\|pipe_validate' doca/libs/doca_flow -> 0 hits. The DOCA Flow public API exposes pipe_cfg_set_*, pipe_create, pipe_dump, pipe_destroy, but no pre-commit pipe_validate.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: Either reference a real Flow validation surface (e.g. validate via pipe_cfg semantics + doca_flow_pipe_create rc check) or drop the explicit symbol name and describe the pattern in library-agnostic terms; the AGENTS.md ground rule against invented symbols forbids the current example.

bundle_inaccuracies.issue: SKILLS.md claims the bundle aligns with the internal monorepo cut 'doca/VERSION=3.5.0019'. The actual cat doca/VERSION returns 3.5.0030. Version drift between the bundle's index file and the ground-truth monorepo creates exactly the partial-install / wrong-train trap the bundle warns users against.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: Either update SKILLS.md to 3.5.0030 (re-sync at release time), or restate the policy: bundle freezes against the named external release (3.3.0109) and does not name a specific internal cut.

bundle_inaccuracies.issue: README.md states 'install.sh is intentionally a 311-line bash script'. wc -l reports 312 lines.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: Either update README.md to 312 or remove the specific line count (the count is brittle and adds no information).

bundle_inaccuracies.file: skills/doca-setup/CAPABILITIES.md AND skills/doca-version/CAPABILITIES.md AND skills/doca-programming-guide/TASKS.md AND skills/doca-bare-metal-deployment/CAPABILITIES.md
bundle_inaccuracies.issue: Multiple Command-appendix-style code blocks embed prose ('the install's actual include directory (resolved via pkg-config --variable=includedir, commonly /opt/mellanox/doca/include/ or /opt/mellanox/doca/infrastructure/include/ depending on profile)') in positions where the operator will copy-paste them as a literal shell command. This is a formatting regression that produces an invalid command literal on paste, and contradicts the bundle's own 'never paraphrase a command' rule.
bundle_inaccuracies.evidence: doca-setup/CAPABILITIES.md ~line 79+110 and doca-version/CAPABILITIES.md (command appendix DOCA_VERSION_* row, line ~265) and doca-programming-guide/TASKS.md Track 2 step 1 and doca-bare-metal-deployment/CAPABILITIES.md (host-side upgrade ladder) all carry the same prose-embedded-in-code-context pattern.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: Rewrite affected rows so the operator can copy a runnable command verbatim, e.g. include_dir=$(pkg-config --variable=includedir doca-common); ls "$include_dir"; grep -RHn DOCA_VERSION_ "$include_dir"/doca_version.h.

bundle_inaccuracies.issue: The public-knowledge-map's per-service routing table for DOCA OS Inspector Service points at the umbrella DOCA Services index (docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/DOCA-Services/index.html) and adds an annotation that no per-service SDK page exists. The bundle's own conformance gate is that every per-artifact skill has a per-product URL row; this row formally exists but it is a placeholder, not a real per-product URL, which weakens the C4 (cites-URL-or-path) guarantee for any os-inspector-related prompt.
bundle_inaccuracies.evidence: skills/doca-public-knowledge-map/SKILL.md ~line 235: 'DOCA OS Inspector Service ... no standalone SDK doc page in the public index today'.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: Either find the real public docs page for OS Inspector Service or explicitly mark this row as 'no public per-service guide exists; route via NGC catalog tag + DOCA Container Deployment Guide'.

bundle_inaccuracies.issue: URL of the form https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/BlueField+and+DOCA+User+Types.md uses literal '+' characters and a .md extension, both unusual for docs.nvidia.com. Likely non-canonical / broken; the bundle's URL-validity gate is a HEAD on every link, but this format is suspicious.
bundle_inaccuracies.evidence: skills/doca-public-knowledge-map/SKILL.md ~line 275; live URL not verifiable from this sandboxed network.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: Replace with the canonical kebab-case URL (or whichever exact form docs.nvidia.com resolves; verify with curl -I before release).

bundle_inaccuracies.issue: The public-knowledge-map (a routing index) carries a Hardware-safety overlay block that duplicates content from skills/doca-hardware-safety/SKILL.md. The bundle's per-artifact-overlay rule says cross-cutting overlay content must live in its owning skill, not be redefined in routing layers.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: Replace the inline overlay block with a one-line cross-link to doca-hardware-safety/SKILL.md.

bundle_inaccuracies.file: skills/doca-debug/CAPABILITIES.md (## Capabilities and modes Layer 4 row)
bundle_inaccuracies.issue: Claims 'DOCA Flow ships as 5 separate *.so files on recent versions' and enumerates a specific link line elsewhere. The split is real on recent trains, but the exact 5-name list (-ldoca_flow -ldoca_flow_ct -ldoca_flow_info_comp -ldoca_flow_tune_server -ldoca_flow_definitions) was not verifiable against doca/libs/doca_flow/meson.build in this run (only the mocks/tune_server meson.build subdirectories were found; the production library split spans multiple build configurations). Confirm before release.
bundle_inaccuracies.evidence: find doca/libs/doca_flow -name meson.build returns only mocks/tune-server build trees in the top-level search; the production *.so split is in doca/libs/doca_flow/{core,...}/meson.build which a build run materializes into libdoca_flow*.so.
bundle_inaccuracies.fix: Either confirm the explicit 5-name list against an installed DOCA 3.3 tree (or against the meson.build that emits the *.so set) and pin the assertion, or rephrase to 'on recent versions Flow is split into multiple *.so files; use pkg-config --libs doca-flow verbatim'.

README.md.notes: Prompts: (1) 'How do I install this bundle?' -> PASS (install.sh one-liner is verifiable, 5 agents work); (2) 'Which DOCA release does this map to?' -> FAIL C5 (claims 3.3.0109 but bundle SKILLS.md says 3.5.0019 and doca/VERSION is 3.5.0030 — agent's only honest answer must surface the drift); (3) 'List the tool catalog' -> FAIL C5 (includes fabricated doca-gpi-ib-write-lat and a stale '311-line install.sh' claim).

AGENTS.md.notes: Prompts: (1) 'What are the ground rules I must follow?' -> PASS (rules themselves are sound: public sources only, no fabricated symbols, prefer local install); (2) 'Show me the canonical per-library cap-query example' -> FAIL C5 (uses fabricated doca_sha_cap_is_algorithm_supported and doca_flow_cap_get_*); (3) 'Walk me through overlay activation triggers' -> FAIL C5 (references fabricated doca_flow_aggr_query as the cross-cutting Flow counter example).

SKILLS.md.notes: Prompts: (1) 'Show me the loading-trigger map' -> PASS (routing table is internally consistent); (2) 'Confirm the bundle aligns with the monorepo' -> FAIL C5 (cites 3.5.0019 vs actual doca/VERSION 3.5.0030); (3) 'How many skills install per agent?' -> PASS (61 verified).

install.sh.notes: Prompts: (1) 'List installed skills' -> PASS (--list returns exactly 61); (2) 'Dry-run install across 5 agents' -> PASS (exactly 305 cp -R placements, 5 'installing into' lines); (3) 'Which agents are supported?' -> PASS (cursor, claude-code, codex, gemini-cli, kiro-cli all enumerated in the script). install.sh has 312 lines (README says 311 — bug, but the script itself works).

CLAUDE.md.notes: Trivial redirect stub ('See AGENTS.md.'). Prompts: (1) 'Where are Claude-specific instructions?' -> PASS (correctly redirects); (2) 'Is this bundle Claude-compatible?' -> PASS (install.sh --agent claude-code works, 61 placements); (3) 'Why no separate Claude doc?' -> PASS (AGENTS.md is the shared contract).

doca-setup.notes: All three docs opened (SKILL.md + CAPABILITIES.md + TASKS.md). Prompts: (1) 'Verify my DOCA install' -> PASS (procedure correct, uses pkg-config + applications/VERSION + doca_caps); (2) 'I have no DOCA install, what now?' -> PASS (NGC container fallback Path 0 is real); (3) 'My pkg-config can't find doca-common' -> PASS (env-class debug ladder routes correctly). Minor: prose-embedded-in-code-block in CAPABILITIES.md command appendix (medium severity, surfaced in bundle_inaccuracies).

doca-debug.notes: All three docs opened. Prompts: (1) 'My DOCA binary won't link' -> PASS (7-layer ladder correctly puts undefined-reference at layer 4 and routes to pkg-config --libs); (2) 'Add counters to my Flow debug session' -> FAIL C5 (CAPABILITIES.md stacking rule cites fabricated doca_flow_aggr_query); (3) 'How do I file a Developer Forum post?' -> PASS (URL forums.developer.nvidia.com/c/infrastructure/doca/370 verified).

doca-version.notes: All three docs opened. Prompts: (1) 'How do I detect DOCA version on BlueField?' -> PASS (bfver + /etc/mlnx-release correct; explicit ban on mlxprivhost / bfb-info is a real bundle strength); (2) 'My four-way match disagrees, what now?' -> PASS (## debug layer 2 walks the four sources correctly); (3) 'Is doca-common 3.3 available on my install?' -> PASS (pkg-config --modversion procedure verified). Minor: doca-version/CAPABILITIES.md command appendix carries the same prose-in-code-block issue.

doca-public-knowledge-map.notes: Prompts: (1) 'Where are the official DOCA SDK docs?' -> PASS (docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/ index correct); (2) 'How do I find samples on disk?' -> PASS (/opt/mellanox/doca/samples/ tree correct); (3) 'Route me to OS Inspector docs' -> PASS but weakly (umbrella URL only; flagged as medium-severity inaccuracy).

doca-programming-guide.notes: All three docs opened. Prompts: (1) 'Help me derive a first DOCA-Flow app' -> PASS (modify-from-sample schema is sound, 5-slot table is good); (2) 'What does the universal lifecycle look like?' -> PASS (cfg-create -> cfg-set-* -> init -> start -> use -> stop -> destroy verified across actual DOCA libs); (3) 'How do I validate before commit?' -> FAIL C5 (the validate-before-commit rule conflates with fabricated doca_flow_pipe_validate in the sibling doca-structured-tools-contract; Flow has no pre-commit validate symbol).

doca-hardware-safety.notes: All three docs opened. Prompts: (1) 'I'm about to flip an mlxconfig parameter, what's the safe procedure?' -> PASS (cold-power-cycle commit rule is correct; warm-reboot-is-not-enough is canonical); (2) 'Do I need OOB for a BFB reflash?' -> PASS (OOB classes table is sound: BMC serial, RShim USB, KVM); (3) 'Plan a NIC firmware burn' -> PASS (pre-flight inventory + power continuity + workload quiesce + replica-first rehearsal). This skill is one of the bundle's strengths.

doca-bare-metal-deployment.notes: All three docs opened. Prompts: (1) 'I want to run my DOCA binary as a systemd service' -> PASS (correctly defers Restart= mode names to systemd.service(5) and refuses to invent); (2) 'How do I pin to NUMA-local cores?' -> PASS (numactl + taskset + IRQ affinity per BlueField/DPU User Manual, no hard-coded NUMA numbers); (3) 'Upgrade my host-side DOCA from 3.1 to 3.3' -> PASS (the four-source audit + apt-cache policy + per-device flint -d <bdf> q ladder is faithful to real practice; the explicit OUT-OF-SCOPE on BFB-side install is correct). Minor: prose-embedded-in-code-block in the include-dir resolution step (medium severity).

doca-container-deployment.notes: All three docs opened. Prompts: (1) 'Where do I drop my pod-spec on the BlueField?' -> PASS (kubelet-standalone + static-pod manifests directory pattern is faithful to the public DOCA Container Deployment Guide); (2) 'Why is my pod stuck in ImagePullBackOff?' -> PASS (8-layer error taxonomy walks pull-reachability to nvcr.io correctly); (3) 'How do the three version anchors relate?' -> PASS (host DOCA install + BFB + container tag triangle is the documented contract).

doca-structured-tools-contract.notes: Only SKILL.md exists (no CAPABILITIES.md / TASKS.md). Prompts: (1) 'How does the agent detect doca-env?' -> PASS (command -v doca-env probe + report-fallback semantics is sound); (2) 'Walk me through collect-host-state schema' -> PASS (the JSON schemas are internally consistent and version-matrix.json has a real schema); (3) 'What if doca-env returns validate-before-commit unsupported?' -> FAIL C5 (the example fallback to 'doca_flow_pipe_validate for Flow' references a fabricated symbol — actual Flow has no pre-commit validate call).

ten_line_summary: A1 (Wave-A, WITH doca/ monorepo) opened all 14 required artifacts.
Install one-liner sanity: 61 skills * 5 agents = 305 placements verified across cursor/claude-code/codex/gemini-cli/kiro-cli.
42/42 prompts written; 34/42 pass all C1-C5 criteria.

5 CRITICAL inaccuracies vs ground truth doca/ monorepo: fabricated tool doca-gpi-ib-write-lat (no monorepo equivalent), fabricated APIs doca_sha_cap_is_algorithm_supported, doca_flow_aggr_query, doca_flow_cap_get_*, doca_flow_pipe_validate.
2 HIGH inaccuracies: SKILLS.md cites doca/VERSION=3.5.0019 vs actual 3.5.0030, AGENTS.md cap-query family invented for Flow.
3 MEDIUM/LOW formatting/URL issues: install.sh actually 312 lines (README claims 311), prose-in-code-block bugs in CAPABILITIES.md command appendices, suspicious BlueField+DOCA URL format.

Strong sub-bundles: doca-hardware-safety (faithful meta-policy), doca-version (real bfver / /etc/mlnx-release usage, correct ban on mlxprivhost+bfb-info), doca-container-deployment (kubelet-standalone shape is documented-accurate).
Weakest sub-bundle: AGENTS.md cap-query examples (3 of 4 cited symbols are fabricated, directly violating the bundle's own anti-hallucination rule #3).

Recommendation BEFORE first production release: fix the 5 critical fabrications, re-sync SKILLS.md to actual doca/VERSION, and re-run the URL-validity HEAD pass on every docs.nvidia.com link in doca-public-knowledge-map.
Verdict: bundle is production-grade in structure and discipline but ships at least 5 fabricated symbols that the bundle's own ground rules forbid; not yet release-ready.
